title: 03.10.26 Weekly Reports Public Records Request, Media Requests, and Code Enforcement Services Requests
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE:
TO:
March 13, 2026
Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Esha Singh, Management Assistant
DEPT: City Manager's Office
SUBJECT: Weekly Reports: Public Records Request, Media Requests, and Code
Enforcement Services Requests
This memorandum includes weekly reports from the City Clerk's Office, Strategic
Communications, and Code Enforcement to the Mayor and City Council for February 27, 2026
through March 5, 2026.
The following reports are attached:
• Public records requests received by the City Clerk's Office (attachment A)
• Weekly media requests received by Communications (attachment B)
• Weekly code enforcement service requests received by Code Enforcement (attachment C)

fields.subcategory: Public Records, Media Requests, Code Enforcement
fields.tldr: This memo transmits weekly activity reports from the City Clerk's Office, Strategic Communications, and Code Enforcement to the Mayor and City Council for the period of February 27 through March 5, 2026. The bulk of the attached data consists of public records requests logged by the City Clerk's Office, covering police reports, fire records, building permits, 911 dispatch logs, body-worn camera video, and other categories. The report was run on March 6, 2026, and lists dozens of individual requestors and their specific record types. No budget items, policy decisions, or action items are presented; the memo is purely informational.
fields.key_stats: The public records request log (Attachment A) spans at least 15 pages and includes reference numbers from P017885 through at least P017943, representing over 58 logged requests for the single week of February 27–March 5, 2026. Request types include Police Department Records (majority), Fire Department Records, and General Public Records covering categories such as police reports, background requests, body-worn camera video, building plans/permits, 911 dispatch logs, traffic accidents, fire prevention records, and site/zoning plans.
